explanation: Diagnosis 2.1(a) cervical radiculopathy is confirmed with MRI showing C5-C6 pathology consistent with symptoms (2.3 satisfied). Conservative treatment is met: 7 weeks of PT exceeds the 6-week minimum (2.2(a)); two pharmacotherapy agents from different qualifying classes — naproxen (NSAID) for 6 weeks and gabapentin (neuropathic agent) for 5 weeks — both exceed the 4-week minimum, satisfying 2.2(b) which requires at least two agents; and one cervical ESI satisfies 2.2(c). Physician documentation provides all four required elements (2.4). All Section 2 criteria are satisfied, so authorization is approved.
